text: The surgeon introduces an endo bag into the peritoneal cavity during a laparoscopic cystectomy for a dermoid cyst. The bag is opened and positioned within the pelvis, followed by placing the entire cyst inside the endo bag.

Now what this does is whenever the cystectomy is being performed, all the spillage of contents would occur directly in the bag, which would prevent any spill of any content of the dermoid into the peritoneal cavity and make the chances for having any form of chemical peritonitis absolutely zero. This greatly improves the post-operative pain and aids in the recovery of the patient.

The surgeon makes an incision along the anti-ovarian border of the ovary and gently peels the dermoid cyst away from the cyst wall, maintaining a bloodless dissection plane. After completing the cystectomy, the cyst wall is opened inside the endo bag, revealing sebaceous content and hair being expressed.

The surgeon ensures that all cyst contents remain contained within the endo bag. The endo bag is then carefully removed from the peritoneal cavity, completing the surgical procedure without any spillage into the peritoneum.
